caption:
So, they say teachers are just saviors of knowledge, but I know better. Tonight, the lecture hall was empty, save for the hum of fluorescent lights and the thump of my own heart 💓. Professor Elena—me—was waiting for stragglers to leave, but one student stayed behind, eyes locked on mine. He didn't ask for homework. He asked for a private review. As I smoothed my silk blouse and felt the fabric cling to my curves, I knew this wouldn't be about calculus. The walk to the faculty lounge was a blur of shadows and whispers. We found a corner where the leather chairs smelled of old books and desire 📚🍷. He ran his hand along my skirt, teasing the hem just enough to make me shiver. 'You always know exactly what you want,' he murmured against my neck. I leaned into his touch, letting my blazer slip slightly, exposing the bare skin between the fabric and my collarbone. The air grew heavy with electricity, the quiet of the university campus outside contrasting with the heat we shared here. When the bell finally rang, it wasn't for class—it was for our next chapter. 🌙✨
